records.ety: [OF. chaldron, F. chaudron kettle. The same word as caldron.]

defs: An English dry measure, being, at London, 36 bushels heaped up, or its equivalent weight, and more than twice as much at Newcastle. Now used exclusively for coal and coke.
notes: ☞ In the United States the chaldron is ordinarily 2,940 lbs, but at New York it is 2,500 lbs.
